sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Michelle, presents with a history of injuring her right knee while skiing in Vermont last weekend.
She fell and her knee popped, and she has been experiencing pain and instability since then.
She was unable to bear weight and had to be taken down by the ski patrol.
She reports pain on the outside of her knee and feels like it won't hold her weight.
She is unable to bend or straighten her knee fully, but can straighten it somewhat with pain.
She denies numbness or tingling in her toes and can feel her toes.
She is a 4-year-old mother of twins and has a supportive husband who is able to help with childcare.
She is generally healthy and denies any chronic medical conditions such as high blood pressure or diabetes.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: On examination, the patient has edema and an effusion over the right knee.
There is pain to palpation of the right lateral knee, decreased flexion and extension, a positive Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse.
There is no leg edema in the ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I suspect a possible rupture of the anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in the patient's right knee based on the history and physical examination.
Problem list: ACL injury; suspected; confirmed by MRI pending.
Plan:
I will order a knee MRI to confirm the diagnosis.
I will continue the patient on ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
I will also put the patient in a knee brace to provide support.
The patient will follow up with me in a few days to discuss the results of the MRI and further management.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in a few days to discuss the results of the MRI and further management.
